{{神社 |名称 = 守山八幡宮 |画像 = |所在地 = 熊本県宇城市小川町南部田1108 |位置 = |祭神 = 応神天皇
神功皇后
姫大神 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = 1051年（永承6年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = }} '''守山八幡宮'''（もりやまはちまんぐう）は、[[熊本県]][[宇城市]][[小川町 (熊本県)|小川町]]にある[[神社]]。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は郷社。 == 概略 == 宇城市小川町を、ほぼ南北に走る日岳山脈の主峰である日岳（標高243m）のふもとに鎮座する神社。由緒は古く、[[宇佐八幡宮]]の末社であったと伝えられるが小川町史編纂委員会編纂『小川町史』[[小川町 (熊本県)|小川町]]役場（熊本県）、1979、[[1051年]]（[[永承]]6年）、[[男山八幡宮]]（石清水八幡宮）の末社として新装復興した年を創建年としている[[熊本日日新聞社]]編纂『熊本県大百科辞典』熊本日日新聞社、1982。[[1048年]]（永承3年）から1051年(永承6年）にかけて社殿が造営され、男山八幡宮の社人高橋宗願が代官となって神幸を奉修した。 '''黄檗版大蔵経'''（'''鉄眼版'''）で知られる[[江戸時代]]の禅僧・[[鉄眼道光]]は、守山八幡宮の社僧・佐伯浄信の子として生まれた。 === 合祀 === *南小野神社も合祀されている。なお、南小野神社は、[[1910年]]([[明治]]43年)に熊野座神社と合併している。 == 御祭神 == *[[応神天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]、姫大神の3柱 == 祭祀・信仰 == *例祭日は、10月15日 == 参考文献 == *上山春平他著『日本神社総覧』[[新人物往来社]]、1992 == 関連項目 == *[[宇佐八幡宮]] *[[石清水八幡宮]] *[[鉄眼道光]] == 脚注 == {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:熊本県の神社]] [[Category:熊本県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宇城市]]

{{神社 |名称 = 守山八幡宮 |画像 = |所在地 = 熊本県宇城市小川町南部田1108 |位置 = |祭神 = 応神天皇
神功皇后
姫大神 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = 1051年（永承6年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = }} '''守山八幡宮'''（もりやまはちまんぐう）は、[[熊本県]][[宇城市]][[小川町 (熊本県)|小川町]]にある[[神社]]。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は郷社。 == 概略 == 宇城市小川町を、ほぼ南北に走る日岳山脈の主峰である日岳（標高243m）のふもとに鎮座する神社。由緒は古く、[[宇佐八幡宮]]の末社であったと伝えられるが小川町史編纂委員会編纂『小川町史』[[小川町 (熊本県)|小川町]]役場（熊本県）、1979、[[1051年]]（[[永承]]6年）、[[男山八幡宮]]（石清水八幡宮）の末社として新装復興した年を創建年としている[[熊本日日新聞社]]編纂『熊本県大百科辞典』熊本日日新聞社、1982。[[1048年]]（永承3年）から1051年(永承6年）にかけて社殿が造営され、男山八幡宮の社人高橋宗願が代官となって神幸を奉修した。 '''黄檗版大蔵経'''（'''鉄眼版'''）で知られる[[江戸時代]]の禅僧・[[鉄眼道光]]は、守山八幡宮の社僧・佐伯浄信の子として生まれた[[源了円]]『鉄眼 - 仮字法語・化縁之疏』[[講談社]]、1994、9頁 === 合祀 === *南小野神社も合祀されている。なお、南小野神社は、[[1910年]]([[明治]]43年)に熊野座神社と合併している。 == 御祭神 == *[[応神天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]、姫大神の3柱 == 祭祀・信仰 == *例祭日は、10月15日 == 参考文献 == *上山春平他著『日本神社総覧』[[新人物往来社]]、1992 == 関連項目 == *[[宇佐八幡宮]] *[[石清水八幡宮]] *[[鉄眼道光]] == 脚注 == {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:熊本県の神社]] [[Category:熊本県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宇城市]]

{{神社 |名称 = 守山八幡宮 |画像 = |所在地 = 熊本県宇城市小川町南部田1108 |位置 = |祭神 = 応神天皇

{{神社 |名称 = 守山八幡宮 |画像 = |所在地 = 熊本県宇城市小川町南部田1108 |位置 = |祭神 = 応神天皇
神功皇后
姫大神 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = 1051年（永承6年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = }} '''守山八幡宮'''（もりやまはちまんぐう）は、[[熊本県]][[宇城市]][[小川町 (熊本県)|小川町]]にある[[神社]]。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は郷社。 == 概略 == 宇城市小川町を、ほぼ南北に走る日岳山脈の主峰である日岳（標高243m）のふもとに鎮座する神社。由緒は古く、[[宇佐八幡宮]]の末社であったと伝えられるが小川町史編纂委員会編纂『小川町史』[[小川町 (熊本県)|小川町]]役場（熊本県）、1979、[[1051年]]（[[永承]]6年）、[[男山八幡宮]]（石清水八幡宮）の末社として新装復興した年を創建年としている[[熊本日日新聞社]]編纂『熊本県大百科辞典』熊本日日新聞社、1982。[[1048年]]（永承3年）から1051年(永承6年）にかけて社殿が造営され、男山八幡宮の社人高橋宗願が代官となって神幸を奉修した。 '''鉄眼一切経'''（黄檗版[[大蔵経]]（鉄眼版））で知られる[[江戸時代]]の禅僧・[[鉄眼道光]]は、守山八幡宮の社僧・佐伯浄信の子として生まれた[[源了円]]『鉄眼 - 仮字法語・化縁之疏』[[講談社]]、1994、9頁。なおその生家は、鉄眼道光自身により後に[[三宝寺 (宇城市)|三宝寺]]と名付けられ、守山八幡宮に隣接している。 == 御祭神 == *[[応神天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]、姫大神の3柱 === 合祀 === *南小野神社も合祀されている。なお、南小野神社は、[[1910年]]（[[明治]]43年）に熊野座神社と合併している。 **南小野神社と熊野座神社の御祭神 ::（五十猛命。王位姫命。伊弉冉命。事解之男命。速玉男命。神功皇后） == 祭祀・信仰 == *例祭日は、10月15日 == 参考文献 == *上山春平他著『日本神社総覧』[[新人物往来社]]、1992 == 関連項目 == *[[宇佐八幡宮]] *[[石清水八幡宮]] *[[鉄眼道光]] == 脚注 == {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:熊本県の神社]] [[Category:熊本県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宇城市]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

姫大神 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = [[1051年]]（永承6年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = }} '''守山八幡宮'''（もりやまはちまんぐう）は、[[熊本県]][[宇城市]]小川町南部田にある[[神社]]である。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は郷社。 == 概略 == ほぼ南北に走る日岳山脈の主峰である日岳（標高243m）のふもとに鎮座する神社。由緒は古く、[[宇佐八幡宮]]の末社であったと伝えられるが小川町史編纂委員会編纂 『小川町史』 [[小川町 (熊本県)|小川町]]役場（熊本県）、1979、[[1051年]]（[[永承]]6年）、[[男山八幡宮]]（石清水八幡宮）の末社として新装復興した年を創建年としている[[熊本日日新聞社]]編纂『熊本県大百科辞典』熊本日日新聞社、1982。[[1048年]]（永承3年）から1051年(永承6年）にかけて社殿が造営され、男山八幡宮の社人高橋宗願が代官となって神幸を奉修した。 '''鉄眼一切経'''（黄檗版[[大蔵経]]（鉄眼版））で知られる[[江戸時代]]の禅僧・[[鉄眼道光]]は、守山八幡宮の社僧・佐伯浄信の子として生まれた[[源了円]]『鉄眼 - 仮字法語・化縁之疏』[[講談社]]、1994、9頁。なおその生家は、鉄眼道光自身により後に[[三宝寺 (宇城市)|三宝寺]]と名付けられ、守山八幡宮に隣接している。 == 御祭神 == *[[応神天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]、姫大神の3柱 === 合祀 === * 南小野神社も合祀されている。なお、南小野神社は、[[1910年]]（[[明治]]43年）に熊野座神社と合併している。 ** 南小野神社と熊野座神社の御祭神 ::（五十猛命。王位姫命。伊弉冉命。事解之男命。速玉男命。神功皇后） == 祭祀・信仰 == * 例祭日は、10月15日 == 境内社 == * 稲荷神社（[[宇迦之御魂神]]） == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == * 日本歴史地名大系44 『熊本県の地名』 [[平凡社]]、1985 * 上山春平他著 『日本神社総覧』 [[新人物往来社]]、1992 == 関連項目 == * [[宇佐八幡宮]] * [[石清水八幡宮]] * [[鉄眼道光]] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:熊本県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宇城市の神社]]

姫大神 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = [[1051年]]（永承6年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = }} '''守山八幡宮'''（もりやまはちまんぐう）は、[[熊本県]][[宇城市]]小川町南部田にある[[神社]]である。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は[[氏子調#行政単位と郷社|郷社]]。 == 概略 == ほぼ南北に走る日岳山脈の主峰である日岳（標高243m）のふもとに鎮座する神社。由緒は古く、[[宇佐神宮|宇佐八幡宮]]の末社であったと伝えられるが小川町史編纂委員会編纂 『小川町史』 [[小川町 (熊本県)|小川町]]役場（熊本県）、1979年、[[1051年]]（[[永承]]6年）、[[石清水八幡宮|男山八幡宮]]（石清水八幡宮）の末社として新装復興した年を創建年としている[[熊本日日新聞社]]編纂『熊本県大百科辞典』熊本日日新聞社、1982年、819頁。[[1048年]]（永承3年）から1051年(永承6年）にかけて社殿が造営され、男山八幡宮の社人高橋宗願が代官となって神幸を奉修した。 '''鉄眼一切経'''（黄檗版[[大蔵経]]（鉄眼版））で知られる[[江戸時代]]の禅僧・[[鉄眼道光]]は、守山八幡宮の社僧・佐伯浄信の子として生まれた[[源了円]]『鉄眼 - 仮字法語・化縁之疏』[[講談社]]、1994、9頁。なおその生家は、鉄眼道光自身により後に[[三宝寺 (宇城市)|三宝寺]]と名付けられ、守山八幡宮に隣接している。 == 祭神 == *[[応神天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]、姫大神の3柱 === 合祀 === * 南小野神社（旧村社）も合祀されている。なお、南小野神社は、[[1910年]]（[[明治]]43年）に熊野座神社と合併している。 ** 南小野神社と熊野座神社の御祭神 ::（五十猛命。王位姫命。伊弉冉命。事解之男命。速玉男命。神功皇后） == 祭祀・信仰 == * 例祭日は、10月15日 == 境内社 == * 稲荷神社（[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂神]]） == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == * 日本歴史地名大系44 『熊本県の地名』 [[平凡社]]、1985年、645頁 * 角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典43 熊本県』[[角川書店]]、1987年、1082頁 == 関連項目 == * [[宇佐神宮]] * [[石清水八幡宮]] * [[鉄眼道光]] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:熊本県の神社]] [[Category:宇城市]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:熊本県の旧郷社]]

revision.comment: Saigen Jiro がページ「[[守山八幡宮]]」を「[[守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]]」に移動しました: 静岡県伊豆の国市に同名神社が存在するため。

revision.text:
[[Category:New pages most likely ugoodbye $1 or similar]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] {{Shrine |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = {{ill|File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|ja|守山八幡宮|lt=Moriyama Hachiman-gu|lt_ja=守山八幡宮}}|size=260px |所在地 = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}} {{ill|Uki City|ja|宇城市}} {{ill|Ogawa Town|ja|小川町}} Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Ojin Tennō|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Jingū Kōgō|ja|神功皇后}}
{{ill|Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = {{ill|1051 AD|ja|1051年}} (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}} |神事 = }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|ja|熊本県}}. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮}}, but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮}} in {{ill|1051 AD|ja|1051年}} (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between {{ill|1048 AD|ja|1048年}} (Eishō 3) and {{ill|1051 AD|ja|1051年}} (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk {{ill|Tetsugan Dōkō|ja|鉄眼道光}} was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * {{ill|Ojin Tennō|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Jingū Kōgō|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社}} in {{ill|1910 AD|ja|1910年}} (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命}}, and {{ill|Jingū Kōgō|ja|神功皇后}}. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}}. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂神}} is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店}}, 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} * {{ill|Tetsugan Dōkō|ja|鉄眼道光}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Uki City]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Former Local Shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uki City]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] [[Category:New pages most likely ugoodbye $1 or similar]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)}} [[Category:New]]

Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮}}, but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮}} in {{ill|1051 AD|ja|1051st year }} (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between {{ill|1048 AD|ja|1048th year }} (Eishō 3) and {{ill|1051 AD|ja|1051st year }} (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk {{ill|Tetsugan Dōkō|ja|鉄眼道光}} was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|ja|講談社}}|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * {{ill|Ojin Tennō|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Jingū Kōgō|ja|神功皇后}} * {{ill|Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社}} in {{ill|1910 AD|ja|1910th year }} (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命}}, and {{ill|Jingū Kōgō|ja|神功皇后}}. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日}}. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂神}} is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, {{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}, 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|ja|角川書店}}, 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * {{ill|Usa Shrine|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} * {{ill|Tetsugan Dōkō|ja|鉄眼道光}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Uki City]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Former Local Shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uki City]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]]

{{神社 |名称 = 守山八幡宮 |画像 = [[File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|260px]] |所在地 = 熊本県宇城市小川町南部田1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 応神天皇
神功皇后
姫大神 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = [[1051年]]（永承6年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = }} '''守山八幡宮'''（もりやまはちまんぐう）は、[[熊本県]][[宇城市]]小川町南部田にある[[神社]]である。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 概略 == ほぼ南北に走る日岳山脈の主峰である日岳（標高243m）のふもとに鎮座する神社。由緒は古く、[[宇佐神宮|宇佐八幡宮]]の末社であったと伝えられるが小川町史編纂委員会編纂 『小川町史』 [[小川町 (熊本県)|小川町]]役場（熊本県）、1979年、[[1051年]]（[[永承]]6年）、[[石清水八幡宮|男山八幡宮]]（石清水八幡宮）の末社として新装復興した年を創建年としている[[熊本日日新聞社]]編纂『熊本県大百科辞典』熊本日日新聞社、1982年、819頁。[[1048年]]（永承3年）から1051年(永承6年）にかけて社殿が造営され、男山八幡宮の社人高橋宗願が代官となって神幸を奉修した。 '''鉄眼一切経'''（黄檗版[[大蔵経]]（鉄眼版））で知られる[[江戸時代]]の禅僧・[[鉄眼道光]]は、守山八幡宮の社僧・佐伯浄信の子として生まれた[[源了円]]『鉄眼 - 仮字法語・化縁之疏』[[講談社]]、1994、9頁。なおその生家は、鉄眼道光自身により後に[[三宝寺 (宇城市)|三宝寺]]と名付けられ、守山八幡宮に隣接している。 == 祭神 == *[[応神天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]、姫大神の3柱 === 合祀 === * 南小野神社（旧村社）も合祀されている。なお、南小野神社は、[[1910年]]（[[明治]]43年）に熊野座神社と合併している。 ** 南小野神社と熊野座神社の御祭神 **:（五十猛命。王位姫命。伊弉冉命。事解之男命。速玉男命。神功皇后） == 祭祀・信仰 == * 例祭日は、10月15日 == 境内社 == * 稲荷神社（[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂神]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 参考文献 == * 日本歴史地名大系44 『熊本県の地名』 [[平凡社]]、1985年、645頁 * 角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典43 熊本県』[[角川書店]]、1987年、1082頁 == 関連項目 == * [[宇佐神宮]] * [[石清水八幡宮]] * [[鉄眼道光]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:熊本県の神社]] [[Category:宇城市の歴史]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:熊本県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宇城市の建築物]]

神功皇后
姫大神 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = [[1051年]]（永承6年） |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月15日 |神事 = }} '''守山八幡宮'''（もりやまはちまんぐう）は、[[熊本県]][[宇城市]]小川町南部田にある[[神社]]である。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は[[郷社]]。 == 概略 == ほぼ南北に走る日岳山脈の主峰である日岳（標高243m）のふもとに鎮座する神社。由緒は古く、[[宇佐神宮|宇佐八幡宮]]の末社であったと伝えられるが小川町史編纂委員会編纂 『小川町史』 [[小川町 (熊本県)|小川町]]役場（熊本県）、1979年、[[1051年]]（[[永承]]6年）、[[石清水八幡宮|男山八幡宮]]（石清水八幡宮）の末社として新装復興した年を創建年としている[[熊本日日新聞社]]編纂『熊本県大百科辞典』熊本日日新聞社、1982年、819頁。[[1048年]]（永承3年）から1051年(永承6年）にかけて社殿が造営され、男山八幡宮の社人高橋宗願が代官となって神幸を奉修した。 '''鉄眼一切経'''（黄檗版[[大蔵経]]（鉄眼版））で知られる[[江戸時代]]の禅僧・[[鉄眼道光]]は、守山八幡宮の社僧・佐伯浄信の子として生まれた[[源了円]]『鉄眼 - 仮字法語・化縁之疏』[[講談社]]、1994、9頁。なおその生家は、鉄眼道光自身により後に[[三宝寺 (宇城市)|三宝寺]]と名付けられ、守山八幡宮に隣接している。 == 祭神 == *[[応神天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]、姫大神の3柱 === 合祀 === * 南小野神社（旧村社）も合祀されている。なお、南小野神社は、[[1910年]]（[[明治]]43年）に熊野座神社と合併している。 ** 南小野神社と熊野座神社の御祭神 **:（五十猛命。王位姫命。伊弉冉命。事解之男命。速玉男命。神功皇后） == 祭祀・信仰 == * 例祭日は、10月15日 == 境内社 == * 稲荷神社（[[ウカノミタマ|宇迦之御魂神]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 参考文献 == * 日本歴史地名大系44 『熊本県の地名』 [[平凡社]]、1985年、645頁 * 角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会編『角川日本地名大辞典43 熊本県』[[角川書店]]、1987年、1082頁 == 関連項目 == * [[宇佐神宮]] * [[石清水八幡宮]] * [[鉄眼道光]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:もりやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:熊本県の神社]] [[Category:宇城市の歴史]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:熊本県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宇城市の建築物]]

Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{Shrine |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = {{ill|File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|ja|守山八幡宮|lt=Moriyama Hachiman-gu|lt_ja=守山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|qq=}}|size=260px |所在地 = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki City]] [[:en:Ogawa, Saitama|Ogawa Town]] Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |神事 = }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman Shrine]], but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|qq=}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between [[:en:1048|1048 AD]] (Eishō 3) and [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|qq=}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|qq=}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|qq=}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|qq=}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|qq=}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|qq=}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (宇城市)|version=87926744|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Uki City]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uki City]] }} [[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]]

[[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]

Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |神事 = }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman Shrine]], but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|qq=}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between [[:en:1048|1048 AD]] (Eishō 3) and [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|qq=}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|qq=}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|qq=}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|qq=}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|qq=}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|qq=}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (宇城市)|version=87926744|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Uki City]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Uki City]] }} [[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 Otoko|ja|事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|qq=}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|qq=}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place {{Shrine |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = {{ill|File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|ja|守山八幡宮|lt=Moriyama Hachiman-gu|lt_ja=守山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|qq=}}|size=260px |所在地 = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki City]] [[:en:Ogawa, Saitama|Ogawa Town]] Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |神事 = }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman Shrine]], but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|qq=}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between [[:en:1048|1048 AD]] (Eishō 3) and [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|qq=}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|qq=}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|qq=}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|qq=}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|qq=}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|qq=}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}} is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |神事 = }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman Shrine]], but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮}} in [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between [[:en:1048|1048 AD]] (Eishō 3) and [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|qq=}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (宇城市)|version=87926744|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Uki City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Uki, Kumamoto]] [[Category:宇城市の歴史]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]]

Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|qq=}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |神事 = }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman Shrine]], but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮}} in [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between [[:en:1048|1048 AD]] (Eishō 3) and [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|qq=}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Otoko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11450044]] Wikidata name: Moriyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = {{ill|File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|ja|守山八幡宮|lt=Moriyama Hachiman-gu|lt_ja=守山八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|qq=}}|size=260px |所在地 = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki City]] [[:en:Ogawa, Saitama|Ogawa Town]] Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |神事 = }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman Shrine]], but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮}} in [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between [[:en:1048|1048 AD]] (Eishō 3) and [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|qq=}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (宇城市)|version=87926744|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Uki, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uki, Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]]

tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Uki, Kumamoto]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11450044]] Wikidata name: Moriyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|size=260px |所在地 = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki City]] [[:en:Ogawa, Saitama|Ogawa Town]] Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |神事 = |location = {{ill|Uki|qid=Q782825}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman Shrine]], but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮}} in [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between [[:en:1048|1048 AD]] (Eishō 3) and [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|qq=}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (宇城市)|version=87926744|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Moriyama Hachiman-gu (Uki)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Uki, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uki, Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11450044]] Wikidata name: Moriyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|size=260px |所在地 = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki City]] [[:en:Ogawa, Saitama|Ogawa Town]] Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |神事 = |location = {{ill|Uki|qid=Q782825}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman Shrine]], but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710098}} in [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between [[:en:1048|1048 AD]] (Eishō 3) and [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568194}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典|1=Buddhist text|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1001051}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|qq=|1=Sambō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355119}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (宇城市)|version=87926744|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] }} [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Uki, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uki, Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11450044}}

[[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450044]] Wikidata name: Moriyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|size=260px |所在地 = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki City]] [[:en:Ogawa, Saitama|Ogawa Town]] Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |神事 = |location = {{ill|Uki|WD=Q782825}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman Shrine]], but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710098}} in [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between [[:en:1048|1048 AD]] (Eishō 3) and [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568194}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典|1=Buddhist text|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1001051}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|qq=|1=Sambō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355119}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (宇城市)|version=87926744|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] }} [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Uki, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uki, Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11450044}}

revision.text:
[[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450044]] Wikidata name: Moriyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|size=260px |所在地 = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki City]] [[:en:Ogawa, Saitama|Ogawa Town]] Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |神事 = |location = {{ill|Uki|WD=Q782825}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman Shrine]], but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710098}} in [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between [[:en:1048|1048 AD]] (Eishō 3) and [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568194}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典|1=Buddhist text|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1001051}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|1=Sambō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355119}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (宇城市)|version=87926744|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] }} [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Uki, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uki, Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11450044}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
