[[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450044]] Wikidata name: Moriyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|size=260px |所在地 = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki City]] [[:en:Ogawa, Saitama|Ogawa Town]] Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |神事 = |location = {{ill|Uki|WD=Q782825}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman Shrine]], but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710098}} in [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between [[:en:1048|1048 AD]] (Eishō 3) and [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568194}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典|1=Buddhist text|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1001051}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|1=Sambō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355119}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (宇城市)|version=87926744|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] }} [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450044]] Wikidata name: Moriyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|size=260px |所在地 = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki City]] [[:en:Ogawa, Saitama|Ogawa Town]] Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |神事 = |location = {{ill|Uki|WD=Q782825}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman Shrine]], but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710098}} in [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between [[:en:1048|1048 AD]] (Eishō 3) and [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568194}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典|1=Buddhist text|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1001051}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|1=Sambō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355119}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (宇城市)|version=87926744|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] }} [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] revision.text:
[[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450044]] Wikidata name: Moriyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|size=260px |所在地 = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki City]] [[:en:Ogawa, Saitama|Ogawa Town]] Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |神事 = |location = {{ill|Uki|WD=Q782825}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman Shrine]], but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710098}} in [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between [[:en:1048|1048 AD]] (Eishō 3) and [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568194}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典|1=Buddhist text|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1001051}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|1=Sambō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355119}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (宇城市)|version=87926744|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] }} [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Uki, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uki, Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q11450044}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11450044}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450044]] Wikidata name: Moriyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|size=260px |所在地 = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki City]] [[:en:Ogawa, Saitama|Ogawa Town]] Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |神事 = |location = {{ill|Uki|WD=Q782825}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman Shrine]], but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710098}} in [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between [[:en:1048|1048 AD]] (Eishō 3) and [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568194}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典|1=Buddhist text|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1001051}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|1=Sambō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355119}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated revision.text:
[[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450044]] Wikidata name: Moriyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|size=260px |所在地 = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki City]] [[:en:Ogawa, Saitama|Ogawa Town]] Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |神事 = |location = {{ill|Uki|WD=Q782825}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman Shrine]], but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710098}} in [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between [[:en:1048|1048 AD]] (Eishō 3) and [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568194}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典|1=Buddhist text|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1001051}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|1=Sambō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355119}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (宇城市)|version=87926744|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11450044}} [[Category:Buildings in Uki, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uki, Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450044]] Wikidata name: Moriyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|size=260px |所在地 = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki City]] [[:en:Ogawa, Saitama|Ogawa Town]] Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |神事 = |location = {{ill|Uki|WD=Q782825}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman Shrine]], but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710098}} in [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between [[:en:1048|1048 AD]] (Eishō 3) and [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568194}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典|1=Buddhist text|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1001051}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|1=Sambō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355119}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (宇城市)|version=87926744|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11450044}} [[Category:Buildings in Uki, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uki, Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uki, Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11450044]] Wikidata name: Moriyama-hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|size=260px |所在地 = [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Uki, Kumamoto|Uki City]] [[:en:Ogawa, Saitama|Ogawa Town]] Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]]
[[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |神事 = |location = {{ill|Uki|WD=Q782825}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, [[:en:Kumamoto Prefecture|Kumamoto Prefecture]]. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Hachiman Shrine]], but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710098}} in [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between [[:en:1048|1048 AD]] (Eishō 3) and [[:en:1051|1051 AD]] (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568194}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典|1=Buddhist text|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1001051}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher=[[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]]|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|1=Sambō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355119}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Ojin Tennō]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]] * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:1910|1910 AD]] (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Jingū Kōgō]]. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is [[:en:October 15|October 15]]. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, [[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]], 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]] * [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine]] * [[:en:Tetsugen Doko|Tetsugan Dōkō]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (宇城市)|version=87926744|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11450044}} [[Category:Buildings in Uki, Kumamoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Municipal History of Uki, Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū Kōgō}}. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15}}. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama}} is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|size=260px |所在地 = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Uki, Kumamoto|en|Uki, Kumamoto|WD=Q782825|lt=Uki City}} {{ill|Ogawa, Saitama|en|Ogawa, Saitama|WD=Q935037|lt=Ogawa Town}} Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ojin Tennō}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū Kōgō}}
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = {{ill|1051|en|1051|WD=Q19363|lt=1051 AD}} (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15}} |神事 = |location = {{ill|Uki|WD=Q782825}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Hachiman Shrine}}, but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710098}} in {{ill|1051|en|1051|WD=Q19363|lt=1051 AD}} (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between {{ill|1048|en|1048|WD=Q19359|lt=1048 AD}} (Eishō 3) and {{ill|1051|en|1051|WD=Q19363|lt=1051 AD}} (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568194}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典|1=Buddhist text|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1001051}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk {{ill|Tetsugen Doko|en|Tetsugen Doko|WD=Q435294|lt=Tetsugan Dōkō}} was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|en|Kodansha|WD=Q726081|lt=Kodansha}}|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|1=Sambō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355119}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ojin Tennō}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū Kōgō}} * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910 AD}} (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū Kōgō}}. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15}}. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama}} is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, {{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}, 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|en|Kadokawa Shoten|WD=Q1054765|lt=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine}} * {{ill|Tetsugen Doko|en|Tetsugen Doko|WD=Q435294|lt=Tetsugan Dōkō}} Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568194}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典|1=Buddhist text|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1001051}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk {{ill|Tetsugen Doko|en|Tetsugen Doko|WD=Q435294|lt=Tetsugan Dōkō}} was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|en|Kodansha|WD=Q726081|lt=Kodansha}}|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|1=Sambō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355119}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ojin Tennō}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū Kōgō}} * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910 AD}} (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū Kōgō}}. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15}}. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama}} is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, {{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}, 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū Kōgō}}
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = {{ill|1051|en|1051|WD=Q19363|lt=1051 AD}} (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15}} |神事 = |location = {{ill|Uki|WD=Q782825}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Hachiman Shrine}}, but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710098}} in {{ill|1051|en|1051|WD=Q19363|lt=1051 AD}} (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between {{ill|1048|en|1048|WD=Q19359|lt=1048 AD}} (Eishō 3) and {{ill|1051|en|1051|WD=Q19363|lt=1051 AD}} (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568194}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典|1=Buddhist text|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1001051}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk {{ill|Tetsugen Doko|en|Tetsugen Doko|WD=Q435294|lt=Tetsugan Dōkō}} was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|en|Kodansha|WD=Q726081|lt=Kodansha}}|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|1=Sambō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355119}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ojin Tennō}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū Kōgō}} * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|size=260px |所在地 = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Uki, Kumamoto|en|Uki, Kumamoto|WD=Q782825|lt=Uki City}} {{ill|Ogawa, Saitama|en|Ogawa, Saitama|WD=Q935037|lt=Ogawa Town}} Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ojin Tennō}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū Kōgō}}
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = {{ill|1051|en|1051|WD=Q19363|lt=1051 AD}} (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15}} |神事 = |location = {{ill|Uki|WD=Q782825}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture}}. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Hachiman Shrine}}, but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710098}} in {{ill|1051|en|1051|WD=Q19363|lt=1051 AD}} (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between {{ill|1048|en|1048|WD=Q19359|lt=1048 AD}} (Eishō 3) and {{ill|1051|en|1051|WD=Q19363|lt=1051 AD}} (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568194}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典|1=Buddhist text|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1001051}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk {{ill|Tetsugen Doko|en|Tetsugen Doko|WD=Q435294|lt=Tetsugan Dōkō}} was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|en|Kodansha|WD=Q726081|lt=Kodansha}}|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|1=Sambō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355119}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ojin Tennō}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū Kōgō}} * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910 AD}} (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū Kōgō}}. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15}}. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama}} is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, {{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}, 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia 43: Kumamoto Prefecture』, edited by the Kadokawa Japanese Place Names Encyclopedia Editorial Committee, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|en|Kadokawa Shoten|WD=Q1054765|lt=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1987, p. 1082 == See Also == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Shrine}} * {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine}} * {{ill|Tetsugen Doko|en|Tetsugen Doko|WD=Q435294|lt=Tetsugan Dōkō}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|守山八幡宮 (宇城市)|version=87926744|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11450044}} {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū Kōgō|1=Empress Jingū}}. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}}. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}} is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] [[ja:守山八幡宮 (宇城市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = {{ruby|Moriyama Hachiman-gu|もりやまはちまん宮}} |image = File:Moriyamahachiman.jpg|size=260px |所在地 = {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Uki, Kumamoto|en|Uki, Kumamoto|WD=Q782825|lt=Uki City|1=Uki, Kumamoto}} {{ill|Ogawa, Saitama|en|Ogawa, Saitama|WD=Q935037|lt=Ogawa Town|1=Ogawa, Saitama}} Nanbuta 1108 |位置 = {{coor d|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP-43_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ojin Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū Kōgō|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = {{ill|1051|en|1051|WD=Q19363|lt=1051 AD|1=1051}} (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} |神事 = |location = {{ill|Uki|WD=Q782825|1=Uki, Kumamoto}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Hachiman Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}}, but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710098}} in {{ill|1051|en|1051|WD=Q19363|lt=1051 AD|1=1051}} (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between {{ill|1048|en|1048|WD=Q19359|lt=1048 AD|1=1048}} (Eishō 3) and {{ill|1051|en|1051|WD=Q19363|lt=1051 AD|1=1051}} (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568194}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典|1=Buddhist texts|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1001051}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk {{ill|Tetsugen Doko|en|Tetsugen Doko|WD=Q435294|lt=Tetsugan Dōkō|1=Tetsugen Doko}} was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|en|Kodansha|WD=Q726081|lt=Kodansha|1=Kodansha}}|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|1=Q11355119|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355119}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ojin Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū Kōgō|1=Empress Jingū}} * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910 AD|1=1910}} (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū Kōgō|1=Empress Jingū}}. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}}. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}} is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} == References == * 『Great Japanese Historical Place Names Series 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture』, {{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}, 1985, p. 645 * 『Kadokawa Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Ishikiyam Hachiman Shrine|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} * {{ill|Tetsugen Doko|en|Tetsugen Doko|WD=Q435294|lt=Tetsugan Dōkō|1=Tetsugen Doko}} {{Shinto {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} |社格 = Gōsha (Regional Shrine) |創建 = {{ill|1051|en|1051|WD=Q19363|lt=1051 AD|1=1051}} (Eishō 6) |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} |神事 = |location = {{ill|Uki|WD=Q782825|1=Uki, Kumamoto}} |coordinates = {{Coord|32.594923|N|130.715042|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Moriyama Hachiman-gu''' (守山八幡宮, もりやまはちまん宮) is a Shinto shrine located in Nanbuta, Ogawamachi, Uki City, {{ill|Kumamoto Prefecture|en|Kumamoto Prefecture|WD=Q130308|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}. Under the former Modern Shrine Ranking System, its Rank was Gōsha. == Overview == Situated at the foot of Nichidake (日岳)—the main peak of the Nichidake Mountain Range, which runs nearly north–south and has an elevation of 243 m—this shrine has an ancient origin. Tradition holds that it was once a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Hachiman Shrine|1=Usa Jingū}}, but according to one account it was rebuilt as a subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja|男山八幡宮|lt=Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=石清水八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石清水八幡宮|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710098}} in {{ill|1051|en|1051|WD=Q19363|lt=1051 AD|1=1051}} (Eishō 6).{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Ogawamachi History』|publisher={{ill|Ogawamachi Town Office|ja|小川町役場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawamachi Town Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kumamoto Prefecture)|date=1979}} It is said that between {{ill|1048|en|1048|WD=Q19359|lt=1048 AD|1=1048}} (Eishō 3) and {{ill|1051|en|1051|WD=Q19363|lt=1051 AD|1=1051}} (Eishō 6) the shrine buildings were constructed, with Takahashi Sōgan, a priest from Otokoyama Hachiman Shrine, serving as governor and overseeing the enshrinement ceremonies.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Kumamoto Prefectural Encyclopedia』|publisher={{ill|Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja|熊本日日新聞社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbunsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊本日日新聞|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568194}}|date=1982|page=819}} Known for the Tetsugan Issai-kyō (the Tetsugan version of the {{ill|Great Canon|ja|大蔵経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Canon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 仏典|1=Buddhist texts|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1001051}}, based on the Huangbo edition), the Edo-period Zen monk {{ill|Tetsugen Doko|en|Tetsugen Doko|WD=Q435294|lt=Tetsugan Dōkō|1=Tetsugen Doko}} was born as the son of the shrine priest Saeki Jōshin of Moriyama Hachiman-gu.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=『Tetsugan – A Brief Explanation of Doctrines』|publisher={{ill|Kodansha|en|Kodansha|WD=Q726081|lt=Kodansha|1=Kodansha}}|date=1994|page=9}} Later, Tetsugan Dōkō himself renamed his birthplace as {{ill|Sambō-ji|ja|三宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sambō-ji|1=Q11355119|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355119}}, which now stands adjacent to Moriyama Hachiman-gu. == Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Ojin Tennō|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū Kōgō|1=Empress Jingū}} * {{ill|Himegami|lt=Himegami|lt=Hime Ōkami|ja|姫大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}} === Merged Shrines === * The {{ill|Minamino Shrine|ja|南小野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamino Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a former village shrine) is also merged here. Note that Minamino Shrine merged with {{ill|Kumanoza Shrine|ja|熊野座神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumanoza Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|1910|en|1910|WD=Q2075|lt=1910 AD|1=1910}} (Meiji 43). ** The enshrined deities of Minamino Shrine and Kumanoza Shrine are: {{ill|Isotake no Mikoto|ja|五十猛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isotake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 五十猛神|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=五十猛神|4=zh|5=五十猛神}}, {{ill|Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja|王位姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, {{ill|Kototoki no Otoko|ja|事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kototoki no Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, {{ill|Hayatama Otoko|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama Otoko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現|1=Kumano Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11568925}}, and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Jingū Kōgō|1=Empress Jingū}}. == Worship and Belief == * The annual festival day is {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}}. == Subordinate Shrines == * An Inari shrine dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|1=Ukanomitama}} is located within the shrine grounds. == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} ==
